key_insight: In molecular MPNNs, performance is driven more by how messages are constructed—especially seed initialization and node-edge fusion—than by the complexity of the update step.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark turns MPNN design into a modular engineering problem, giving practical heuristics for choosing operators that improve molecular property prediction without exhaustive architecture search.
method: Factorial benchmark of 84 2D molecular MPNN configurations built from three operator families: message-seed initialization, node-edge fusion, and node update.
Evaluated on ten MoleculeNet datasets under a shared experimental and statistical analysis protocol.
Used representation probing on Quinethazone plus representative configurations to interpret mechanistic effects and compare against GNN baselines.
evidence_signal: Across ten MoleculeNet datasets, message-seed initialization and node-edge fusion showed family-level effects, while the update family did not; representative models ranked numerically best on eight datasets.
watch_item: The strongest conclusions are family-level and dataset-aggregated, so operator advantages may still depend on dataset type, metric, and implementation details.
paper_title: What drives performance in molecular MPNNs? An operator-level factorial benchmark
